entries.title: The Fog of War
entries.content:
Today was supposed to be our final day in the hotel. We had just picked up the keys to our new apartment, and the plan was to go on a nice double date with a pair of friends. Afterwards, we were going to buy furniture so we could finally settle down after four months of hotel living.

However, the world seemed to have another plan.

While we were on a romantic boat ride with our friends, the news started rolling in that the U.S. and Israel had hit Iran, and that Iran was retaliating by firing toward the Gulf. What was supposed to be a romantic boat ride quickly turned into a panicked one—taking phone calls and trying to figure out what this meant for us, but also for our employees on the ground.

Once the boat docked, we headed back to our hotel room and invited our coworkers over so none of us had to experience the chaos alone. We ordered some shawarma and sat together, hanging out in confusion.
entries.newsHeadline: Iranian missiles shake Gulf cities after US, Israeli strikes on Iran

entries.title: Testing entry one
entries.content: A bit tired and mixed emotions due to the uncertainty of the future I’m kind of over hotels. The ai space is moving so fast and so spicy that I really wish I had a home base set up so I can experiment. Update
